compositionCheck.notes: Overall composition matches Figma reference. Charcoal panels display with correct rounded corners, generous inner padding, content is fully visible and well-positioned inside the panels. No content clipping observed at any edge.

focusAreas.figmaValue: 96px vertical padding inside charcoal panel (py-[96px] on Content wrapper)
focusAreas.observation: At 1440w, the charcoal panels show generous padding above the eyebrow text and below the bottom of the carousel cards. The heading area has ample breathing room at the top, and the card bottoms sit well within the charcoal background. The 4px difference from Figma's 96px is at the upper edge of the 2-4px tolerance and visually indistinguishable.

focusAreas.figmaValue: No explicit mobile Figma reference provided for this node (785:33970 is desktop only)
focusAreas.observation: At 375w, the charcoal panels show appropriate padding. Content has clear vertical breathing room inside the charcoal backgrounds. The 72px mobile padding provides a proportionally generous cushion that scales well from the 92px desktop value. No content appears cramped or clipped.

focusAreas.figmaValue: 32px outer margin around charcoal panel (p-[32px] on outer wrapper in Figma)
focusAreas.implementationValue: Section spacing via py-10 sm:py-16 (40px mobile / 64px sm+) between sections. Charcoal pseudo extends 32px beyond container (before:-inset-y-8), so visible gap between charcoal panels = section py minus pseudo extension. At desktop: 64px - 32px = 32px visible gap between adjacent charcoal panels.
focusAreas.difference: 0px at desktop (32px matches Figma outer margin)
focusAreas.observation: Looking at the full-page 1440w screenshot, the gaps between stacked charcoal sections appear consistent and appropriately sized. Each section has a visible strip of the page background between the charcoal panels, providing clear visual separation without excessive whitespace. The gap is neither cramped nor too large -- it creates a comfortable visual rhythm.

focusAreas.area: Content clipping at edges of charcoal panels
focusAreas.observation: No content clipping observed at any breakpoint. At 1440w, all card images, text, category badges, navigation arrows, and pagination bars sit comfortably within the charcoal backgrounds. At 375w, the single-column card layout displays fully within the charcoal area with no overflow or clipping. The horizontal pseudo extension (before:-inset-x-(--side-gutter) on mobile, before:-inset-x-8 on desktop) provides adequate horizontal coverage.
The charcoal panels display with clearly visible large rounded corners at both breakpoints, consistent with the Figma design's 25px radius. The 1px difference is well within tolerance.

overallAssessment.desktop1440: The DynamicContentCarousel at 1440w closely matches the Figma reference (node 785:33970). The charcoal panels provide generous inner padding (~92px vs Figma's 96px, within 4px tolerance). Content sits well within the panels with no clipping. The gap between stacked sections creates clear visual separation (~32px matching Figma). Navigation arrows, pagination bar, and card content are all properly contained.
overallAssessment.mobile375: At 375w, the block adapts well to mobile. The charcoal panels maintain appropriate padding (~72px). Cards stack to single column and display fully within the charcoal background. No content clips at horizontal edges thanks to the side-gutter-based pseudo extension. Section spacing is proportional and comfortable.

notes.observation: The implementation's 92px desktop inner padding vs Figma's 96px is a 4px difference -- right at the boundary of the 2-4px semantic tolerance. While acceptable, if a future revision is needed for other reasons, adjusting to py-16 (64px) + before:-inset-y-8 (32px) = 96px would achieve an exact match. However, this would change the mobile padding to 64+32=96px as well, which may be too much for mobile. The current responsive scaling (72px mobile / 92px desktop) is well-proportioned.
